channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Why the FUCK is life so fucking difficult (particularly the financial aspect of it). — 51 replies — 204.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why the FUCK is life so fucking difficult (particularly the financial aspect of it).

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-04-25 20:05 UTC

OP

Why the FUCK is life so fucking difficult (particularly the financial aspect of it).

Early replies (first 3)

62155115
>>62155108
it's the way capitalism works, it's an annihilationist force that is counter to life. it will continue until we're all dead and earth has been completely liquidated. I dont' know why we do this to ourselves

>uncleted.jpg

62155117
LUCK is the biggest factor in life.

62155122
>>62155115
Just like cancer

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62155530
>>62155108
Others want to make it as difficult as possible for some reason.

62155543
>>62155163
this, capitalism is an egregore. its a force that exists as something greater than the sum of its parts (human brains exchanging labor value). it has grown beyond us and will one day devour us

62155588
>>62155530
Stiffle competition.

62155599
The ages don't line up with what you biologically want to do
>As a kid / teenager
This is where you have the most TVM to benefit you, but you can't have a job and if you got one you're just buying a gaming computer and working part-time
>20's
This is when you have good TVM to benefit from but you're just starting in whatever field, or possibly still in school. A natural human doesn't feel like planning for 40 years in the future here, he just wants to make babies and do cool things.
>Late 20's approaching 30's
This is where you feel like you have to start a business or build something or get married - this happens universally to everyone who still has their natural human instincts. At some point you just see what you needed to do in the past to be successful today, and now it's up to yourself from the past to have the capital to make whatever you want to do now work, but they don't have the same motives as you.
>>62155173
Early 20's, not going all the way to 30's with this.
If you spent like 5 years moneymaxxing and just investing as much as possible that would pretty much set you up for life even in a relatively low paying job, even with a mcjob if you just live with your parents and buy rental properties.
>>62155198
>you could literally live with your parents, collect welfare and then go all in on stocks while eating chicken tendies. how is it difficult? you're lucky you weren't born during the middle ages
Yup, more kids need to be told this. Live with your parents, work 5 years investing everything, you've just saved 20 or 30 years of work later.
It's really simple but instead we have these people told they need to go 100,000 in debt and blow 4-6 years of their life studying, when instead they could make 100,000 in that time frame that's all invested.

62155603
>>62155108
I made 7 million dollars slinging shitcoins and I have a tested IQ of 104. Might be a skill issue.

62155607
>>62155603
You can't even retire on 7 million these days. That's at best 5 years of retirement with today's inflation.

62155612
You feel difficult brcauze you don’t have. Little sister like me who can let me breeding press her everyday when my bag is full.

62155614
>>62155607
You can pseudo-retire on 100k if you live with parents and take up a couple hundred in odd jobs per month. Not a true retirement, more like a "work a day a week" thing.

62155675
>>62155108
in the past (pre 2020) it used to be cause people didnt know how to invest and bought shit they didnt need. now its wealth inequality, they're literally siphoning all producitivity gains

62155705
I find it easy to save and invest, but it’s extraordinarily difficult to find an opportunity, go all-in with size, and do a 10x. In this life, you either accept wagecuckery until 62-65, you hypergamble in the market, or you become an entrepreneur.

62155765
>>62155705
Yeah, 10x's are hard but 2x's just happen consistently, so it's more about doing it when you're younger.
A little bit of wagecuckery chasing 2x's goes a long way when you're 20 and those will double like 4 times before retirement.

62155773
>>62155765
The issue is you see an asset that is going parabolic, you say “yeah, this is it” and then it does 2x, 4x, etc. but the very fucking moment you hop in, you’re down 30 percent. Right now, everyone is getting rich on the AI bottleneck trade. If I wasn’t holding bitcoin, I’d just say fuck it and hop in.

62155777
>>62155198
>you could literally live with your parents, collect welfare and then go all in on stocks while eating chicken tendies. how is it difficult? you're lucky you weren't born during the middle ages
lmao literally me except i dont eat tendies
i am currently investing 100% in ftse all world and while it grows well idk if i can escape the neet life with it. also i can also only save up to 10k before the state cuts my neet money
dont even know what i would do with the money if i got lucky

62155782
>>62155773
I've been holding more cash specifically because of the AI stuff, I still invest but I think some kind of downturn is coming and I want to be able to jump at opportunities.
I really would never go all-in on some kind of "ai bottleneck", it's just gambling. Sure, gamble with 25% if you really want to.

Recently houses have been going for 100k in my area (lockport new york because people will say this is fake) and I'm waiting for an even further dip as boomers die off, which has actually been happening because all land owners in my area are 75.

62155861
>>62155782
The outsized returns come with the gambling. Don’t get me wrong, all of my money is in VOO and bitcoin. But it causes deep distress to see people printing off of leveraged sector ETFs. Real estate is sometimes the move. Homes that were $100k in 2002 are now sometimes $1.3m. But I’ve also seen other areas that were the next big thing, and they’ve crabbed for decades.

My take? New York has had net population losses for a while. Buffalo is doing better in recent years, but will it extend to Lockport? Unsure.

62155878
>>62155115
>>62155122
>>62155117
Can't believe you retards haven't figured out the real answer. I'll give you a hint, it's jews.

62155879
>>62155861
>But will it extend to Lockport?
It already has, but in rural areas. My city is in a decline in population and nobody's making any money here and never will, which is where my edge comes in.
Since I'm not 75 and not literally dying to sell off my shit to pay for a new truck I can get along maintaining and renting houses, and it's no big loss to have one I have to sell because I can do basically all cosmetic work myself.

But people are desperate to escape city shitholes so small suburb areas and even lockport have seen buffalo growth.

62155881
>>62155108
Because of genetic determinism and how the modern world works. We have abundance thanks to modern tech and slave labor from third world countries but everyone wants to have access to the wealth. Meanwhile the state simply prints more money making your money worth less. The only way to counter this is by having assets that are not bound to this or scale higher then inflation. We still have law of nature, the fittest aka the most adaptable wins and will be on top, just that most people who would die normally are being dragged on for some reason.

62155923
>>62155108
just stop caring
i havent done laundry in over a year. my apartment is a disaster and i eat like once every 3-4 days out of pure lazyness.
i have no friends never go out other than work.
i take several stimulants to keep my brain on autopilot at work so im not really "there"
i recently lost the will to commute so i just uber it both ways
when i get home i just rot in bed, look at the numbers move and watch oneyplays until i pass out.
sometimes ill play something on my 'eck if im feeling especially motivated.
i just dont care anymore.

62155978
>>62155108
too many people

62156145
>>62155259
>you are framed and jailed for sexual assault,
kek, definitely a thing that happens to normal, well-adjusted people

62156342
>>62155878
jews are huma....oh wait!

62156367
>>62155198
What to invest in tho?
Nothing gives more than 5% annual returns these days unless you do degenerate options gambling

62156382
>>62155130
>where it was financially optimal to take care of your cows and pigs so that they lived happy lives
This is impossible regardless of how well you treat your livestock. Beef cattle are typically slaughtered when they're somewhere between 1-2 years old. Dairy cattle are slaughtered once their milk production starts to decline, around 5 years old.

The natural lifespan of a cow is 20-30 years. You could treat your cattle incredibly well but you're still killing them when they're basically children. Killing a 1-2 year old cow is equivalent to killing a 6-8 year old human. If you were treated very well for the first 8 years of your life before being slaughtered so some alien species could eat you, would you consider that you lived a happy life?

62156385
>>62155108
in order for the few percentage of rich population to enjoy the leisure time, you need a society of people jsut barely getting by so they need to be at work as much as possible making either you or your rich buddies money. all the while keeping you out of their way for a majority of the week.

62156388
>>62156382
I'd have been eaten in 2000, being a wagecuck in 2026.

62156412
>>62155159
seriously though I have this, idk, risk aversion disability where even if something is 99% likely to make big gains if I just hold for a year, I will constantly panic sell and then cry afterwards. I don't know what the fuck is wrong with me. It's happened like 5 times now.

62156445
>>62155923
>oneyplays

dude go get some help tf

62156449
>>62156367
you have to gamble if you want to win. There are too many people on earth for you to make it without taking on risk.

62156476
>>62155108
Things will never change until the intelligence agencies, underground military-industrial-complex lairs and defense contractor facilities are stormed and the glowniggers and parasites who work there are executed and the data they collect is leaked to the public so that the rest of these government assets, zogbots, mercenaries, glorified jannies and authority figures can be hunted down and exterminated.

item.title: Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the p… — 39 replies — 156.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the p…

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-04-25 20:02 UTC

OP

Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the previous “Buy One, Add One for $1” menu AKA the only reason to have the app anymore after the deals turned to shit. I’ve also noticed with this update the drink prices increased by $0.20, a $0.40 increase YoY in my area. Yesterday I was starving after work but drove straight passed McDonald’s out of pure anger. Went home and ate some eggs and toast.

Thoughts?

Early replies (first 3)

62155716
>>62155708
Give it up, brother. Your fat ass doesn't need to be eating McDick's anyway.

62155723
>>62155708
For me, it's Wendy's. They still have a decent meal deal although it has gone up $1 over the past couple years. Ironically they have been a better value than Micky d's for a little while now.
>>62155716
Sounds like projection tb.h

62155728
>>62155708
learn to cook

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62155749
>>62155739
>Grass-fed beef is 7.50 a pound at walmart.com
Sam's club and aldi have it for $1-2 less per pound.

62155752
>>62155748
That's fucking horrible. Or really good anon. I don't know how your monopoly money works. I pay ~40 for a 10 lb roll from a local restaurant wholesaler

62155761
>>62155749
Yeah I just use shartmart as an example because everyone always says
>BRO I can't find the stuff you're talking about!
And they're always in stock and not even the cheapest so the infinite bitching about prices falls apart when shartmart has a decent price.

62155819
>projection projection projection
You really like that one word, don't you?

Moron.

62155826
>overpay
>to wait in a drive thru line for 20 minutes
>get cold food
yeah I'm thinking its mcfucking over for me

62155831
"projection" is basically "no u" posting for midwit adults that think they're smarter.

62155832
>>62155732
>i eat goyslop to piss off anon!

okay fatty

62155841
>>62155819
>>62155831
>>62155832
Projection.

62155851
>>62155748
To be fair, that's canadian dollars which are worth much less than usd

62155867
>>62155851
Canada and Australia should adopt a different dollar sign. They casually post prices in their shitty cheap plastic "dollars" on the internet like it's USD, and that confuses everyone.

62155874
>>62155708
>Yesterday I was starving after work but drove straight passed McDonald’s out of pure anger.
the goyim have gone insane

62155922
Anyone still eating McDonald’s can go get McFucked. I remember eating there as a kid in the 80s and they used to have events where some pedo used to larp as Ronald McDonald. The playground and food was great too. It was wholesome. The jews ruined that, along with everything else. Pizza Hut was also top tier. Fast food doesn’t deserve our money anymore.

62155943
>>62155708
"Product"

62155947
>>62155922

62155976
>>62155922
>Fast food doesn’t deserve our money anymore.
while the quality of ingredients at least outside the US has improved massively over the years, the overall price-to-value ratio has reached a point where you can get proper food at a restaurant for sometimes less but more often than not the same amount of money.
it just doesn't make sense anymore and too much of it is still bad for you so I guess I agree with you there.

62156013
McD is like the only cheap fast food place at this point.

$7.59 for a large meal. I just checked Wendys for a similar meal and they charge $11.59!

62156034
Will ozempic taken by every fatass in the country make mcdonalds drop their prices or will the influx of thinner people that think they can suddenly eat more make them raise?

62156037
>>62155708
It was $2.12 using the apps 40% off deal to get my one double cheeseburger because I didn't want two for $4, now it's $1.70. This works out better for me as long as they don't remove the app discount.

62156039
>>62155708
ill have 2 large fries no salt
4 mcdoubles extra onion extra ketchup add mayo
4 hot and spicy mcchickens extda mayo extra lettuce
40 mcnuggets all habanero ranch
big mac
uhhhhhhhhhhhh

62156052
>>62155737
>Top post on the FIRE subreddit is complaining about how much the price of taco bell has increased
If Taco Bell impacts your finances that much then you don't have enough to retire

62156090
>he didn’t take profits on the $5 meal deal
ngmi

62156160
>>62155732
Ok but why visit a real restaurant or at least some small business?

62156273
>>62155708
That fucking mcchicken used to be $1. Why is mcniggers still trying to call this shit a “value” menu.

62156289
>>62156273
yeah in 1980 dude. holy fuck for a finance subreddit this place sure does suck sometimes.
picrel is the menu around 1975.

62156291
>>62156273
honestly it should still be a buck, no way that sandwich costs more than 50 cents to make

62156292
>>62156289
the mcchicken was a dollar in the 2010's

62156294
>>62156292
sure, and rent was $500 and employees only made $4 per hour and insurance rates were cheap and franchising costs were cheap.

62156301
>>62156294
Why are you mad and wrong?
>>62156292
This poster is factually accurate. McD is crazy expensive now.

62156370
>>62155708
>crying that his goysyrup went up 20¢

62156472
>>62156160
Because I cannot be in two places at the same time. At least not in my corporeal form.

item.title: I hate poor people — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I hate poor people

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-25 19:55 UTC

OP

As someone that grew up with a dirt poor family and had to learn money literacy growing up (now a milloinaire at 30, from 25) i genuinely fucking hate poor people.

As soon as I got money (got luck on ethereum, both at 8 dollars, sold at 850), my family wanted to cannibalize it right away to buy brand new car, gucci handbags, iphones etc. Legitimately, I was giving so them so much but it wasnt enough.

Thats when I realized whhy they were poor in the first place. They couldve lifted themselves out of poverty but they legitimately have brains like n*ggers and have to IMMEDIATELY spend the money instead of putting it into an ETF etc.

Im so fucking ashamed that I dont talk to my mom anymore but I know its for the best.

At some stage it got so bad that they were just treating me like a an ATM machine.

Im not a sadistic person, but I fucking take pleasure ethat they're living in severe poverty and its all their fault.

Being poor, first and foremost, is a sickness of the mind, before anything else.

Early replies (first 3)

62156128
>>62156120
Same I have cut contact with my entire family and I am much happier just doing what is best for me. They are worthless people and I am ashamed that I have their dna inside me.

62156129
>>62156120
>Brand new cars, iphones, Gucci handbags
How brown are you

62156159
>>62156120
Ok thats sexy and all, but when can i buy this shit under 1500?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156228
>>62156120
So many indicators in your text that their low IQ has been fully passed on to you as well.

62156278
>>62156228
nigga im typing fast chill out

62156345
>>62156120
I used to be like this. it was due to 1) being tired of having nothing. It's easy to sit back and say "leave all you money in an ETF" when you're already comfortable. 2) I didn't know anything about ETFs. I thought the stock market was just gambling.

62156346
> bought at $8 and sold at $850
> They couldve lifted themselves out of poverty
Survivorship bias on full display here.
> BUT I WORKED HARD FOR EVERY LAST CENT OF THAT MONEY!
Move along everyone.

62156391
>>62156346
>> BUT I WORKED HARD FOR EVERY LAST CENT OF THAT MONEY!

Unironically I did. I worked hard for the few thousand bucks, that I then invested/speculated into an emerging asset.

Stay mad. Stay poor.

62156398
>>62156391
He is just mad that he missed the chance and now next chance is ai, well guess what he is missing that as well. You already know why poor people are poor.

62156406
is this a copypasta from 2017. How would you 'become a millionaire from 2021 to 2026' buying ethereum, something which has only 2xed from the 2022 lows? Did you already have 500k or something?

62156409
>>62156346
This is literally just a copypasta. Eth was 8 bucks..... in 2016. OP is posting a 10yo copypasta.

62156411
>>62156346
This.
>>62156391
If you had put it in gold or even a decent stock I'd somewhat agree with you but you literally gambled on crypto and got lucky.

62156434
>>62156406
no, i made a few hundred k from eth when i was 22, i became a millionaire at 25 after some investments

62156443
>>62156411
>If you had put it in gold or even a decent stock I'd somewhat agree with you but you literally gambled on crypto and got lucky.
cope.

item.title: >NW in 2021: $1.3M >NW in 2026: $550k — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>NW in 2021: $1.3M >NW in 2026: $550k

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-25 19:54 UTC

OP

>NW in 2021: $1.3M
>NW in 2026: $550k

Early replies (first 3)

62151602
>>62151595
Why didn't you just invest it all into the shit and piss 500 and retire?

62151615
>>62151602
/thread

62151661
>>62151602
>>62151615
because $1.3M wasn't enough to retire and i didn't think bitcoin had topped yet

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62151668
>>62151661
So why didn't you invest in the shit and piss 500 and keep working?

62151688
>>62151661
Kill yourself.

62151738
>>62151668
i already told you?? everyone including me thought bitcoin was going to go to at least $100k
>>62151688
im already dead inside, don't worry

62151783
And you probably called everyone else an idiot buying into "the inflationary fiat scam". Kys

62152021
>>62151595
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62154513
>>62151602
Unrealized dog coin gains

62156280
>>62151661
you are a fucking megatard.

62156337
>>62151661
1.3M in S&P and get you an almost guaranteed 100k a year gain. easily enough to retire in a low or even medium cost of living area.

62156425
>>62151595
>The average "only total return matters" investor who never realizes their gains experience

62156430
>NW in 2021
20k
>NW in 2026
100k

62156432
what does one do in this situation? Personally I would live minimalist as possible and wait for the DXY to correct, then go all into bitcoin or something

item.title: Once I get like a half a mill I'm just gonna buy and sell the TQQQ dips. — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Once I get like a half a mill I'm just gonna buy and sell the TQQQ dips.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-04-25 19:56 UTC

OP

Once I get like a half a mill I'm just gonna buy and sell the TQQQ dips. They happen fairly often

Early replies (first 3)

62156267
>>62156258
I bought SOXL for 9 dollars last year but had to sell it around 25 bucks to pay for emergency expenses.
Leveraged ETFs seem too good to be true, if I held that it wouldve been 10x in a year, it felt like a no brainer because the demand for semiconductors has been unmet for the last 5 years

62156274
if I had half a million I'd be all in on the next 100x.

62156279
>>62156274
I got 300k so I'm not that far off. Bitcoin going back to ath would really help

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156298
>>62156267
>Leveraged ETFs seem too good to be true
Hype for the next one to drop

62156312
>>62156258
just don't sell wenn it keeps dipping after your buy in. be patient and let it dip hard, don't sweat missing out on a dip

62156323
>>62156279
>300k
Are you the 40yo walmart guy who posted the other day about needing a 10x?

62156341
>>62156258
500K is already a lot of money. It's literally enough to retire, I hope you lose everything on your gambling, Faggot.

62156348
>>62156341
>enough to retire
Nigga please.

62156353
QQQ dropped 37% in 2021. You would have gone to 0. Promise you wouldn’t have survived that “buying the dips in TQQQ”, given the nature of 3x ETFs.

62156358
>>62156323
Lol no but I know who you're talking about

62156361
>>62156353
Just wait and you're fine

62156372
>>62156353
was 2022 actually

62156447
>>62156348
Unless you're a Jew because Jew can't ever retire from jewing.

item.title: help — 14 replies — 43.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
help

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-25 19:46 UTC

OP

I lost 20k out my initial capital of 75k. since 6 weeks shorting BTC.closed half still holding. My only hope trump start bomb iran again. Any secret gems that i can get at least 10x in a week? or anything on polymarket?

Early replies (first 3)

62151162
Why would you short BTC? you understand that Iran takes tolls from ships in BTC or Yuan so this literally proves the utility of bitcoin. Haters will deny this truth, but Bitcoin is an eagerly accepted alternate payment method. Also Saylor has nearly perfected the perpetual motion volatility engine

62151171
>>62151162
>Why would you short BTC?
i trust Bitcoin cycle repeat and global instability

62151185
>>62151148
it crashed at an extreme pace over the past few months, did you really expect another leg down?

And regarding global events, always bet on nothing ever happens, faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62151202
>>62151148
>My only hope trump start bomb iran again
I know making money is a zero sum game and war is maximum profitable but damn it still gets to me that a whole bunch of traders around the world are wishing death upon another nation so their numbers go up.

62151203
>>62151148
>I lost 20k out my initial capital of 75k
>shorting BTC

I love that for you.

62151255
>>62151148
So you wanna lose all of your 75k?

My onle advice is that you market buy LINK and hold

62151340
>>62151148
How the hell happened? I must admit I was also shorting, but it's been such a nice pump I've been able to break even and not lose anything. We're not having any violent candles and we usually dump during the weekend.

Try not to go too crazy with the leverage.

62151344
>>62151148
>help i gambled and lost some money, please tell me which table at the casino to go to next

62151778
>>62151148
>he redeemed
Do not redeem the card sar!

62154519
>>62151148
Lose everything and start a new

62154644
>>62151148
Just buy spot Ether and useful Ethereum tokens. I know you want to copy the genius traders grunting, "Line look like 4 year ago line," but the market is steadily going up.

62154654
>>62151148
I voted for this

62156393
>>62151148
Polymarket won't help you with such high amount of money. I know how to basically guarantee a long term profit on polymarket, but it only works in low liquidity markets.

62156402
I don't know your feel, the most I've ever lost was panic selling bottoms and that was for only 5k in losses back in 2022.

You should consider not shorting after the market has already giga dumped. That would be a smart thing to do.
>secret gems I can 10x in a week
This is how you lose the other 55k.

item.title: I'm rich and wealthy. AMA — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm rich and wealthy. AMA

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-25 19:57 UTC

OP

I'm rich and wealthy. AMA

Early replies (first 3)

62156305
Are you white?

62156311
>>62156305
No, Hispanic.

62156347
>>62156311
Why aren't you white?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156415
what is the difference between having money and having wealth? in your own words plz

62156428
I just sneezed. Are you talking about me?

62156436
I will suck your latina cock for 100k.

62156452
>>62156311
How do I avoid ending up hispanic?

item.title: Weed Stocks — 8 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Weed Stocks

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-25 19:36 UTC

OP

What ones are you bros going into?

I chose ACB, Aurora Cannabis as it's the only one that has made a significant profit this year.

Peak - 1503.00
Currently - 3.34

Early replies (first 3)

62152347
>>62151907
Bump. This board is just crypto gamblers isn't it?

62152480
>>62152347
bullshit, this board is for moni makn

62152857
>>62151907
I made huge bank on Canopy and tilray when Canada went legal.
I think tilray is better posed right now for usa and europe.
usa is still long way off federally.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152931
>>62151907
yeah long personality disorders

62153098
>>62152931
Cope

Epstein even mentions how weed smokers are harder to manipulate.
Tranny redd*tors cause more mental illness then maraijuana

62154452
>>62152931
Yea because pills didn't make people into troons or anything. Sleeping pills went so well.

Stupid goy.

>>62153098
I believe this in a way. You are more cerebral and definitely more heart motivated than head motivated. Pot heads are retards but it's definitely helpful and powerful in the right moments.

62154587
>>62153098
>trusting a manipulator
>>62154452
you guys are coping hard

62156379
>>62152347
its why the board exists so yeah

item.title: Hahahahahahaha — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hahahahahahaha

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-25 20:01 UTC

OP

EAT FUCKING SHIT WEATHERXM
Polymarket is signing a deal with nubila due to the Paris blow dryer event (just confirmed in discord)

We won the fucking weather depin war Marco bros
EAT FUCKING SHIT WEATHERXM YOU OVERPRICED PIECE OF SHIT

Early replies (first 3)

62156455
>>62156453
Hahahahahahaha

62156456
>>62156453
I just spent 2 weeks collecting data and training a model on Chongqing weather station for nothing ...

62156462
>>62156456
Hahaha hahaha
Join us Marco bro
You can buy them at home depot
They have a contract with home depot now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156469
>>62156462
Feels SO FUCKING GOOD

item.title: QUANTUM COMPUTER BREAKS ECC KEY IN BITCOIN-RELATED TEST — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
QUANTUM COMPUTER BREAKS ECC KEY IN BITCOIN-RELATED TEST

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-04-25 19:58 UTC

OP

>A researcher used public quantum hardware to break a 15-bit elliptic curve key, winning Project Eleven’s 1 BTC Q-Day Prize.

>The test does not threaten Bitcoin’s 256-bit security yet, but highlights growing urgency around post-quantum upgrades, with ~6.9M BTC sitting in addresses with exposed public keys.

Why is no one talking about this?

Early replies (first 3)

62156396
>>62156349
because its a pop sci plebbit headline
there are still no quantum computers, and there's a good chance there will never be

62156397
ONE
NOTHIN WRONG WITH ME

62156454
it was a brute force attack not using shor. So not interesting the same way using your laptop break 15 bit ecc is not interesting

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: XRP is the biggest scam ever — 36 replies — 107.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP is the biggest scam ever

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-04-25 19:46 UTC

OP

they sell token to shift money over to the tradfi company Ripple to buy actual companies and assets and don't pay any dividends or yield on actual xrp tokens. I've seen actual xrp baggies say this is somehow a good thing.

Early replies (first 3)

62143238
Gee, wonder what they could buy

62143321
>>62143203
Means I can buy more for less. BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62143438
>>62143203
>ethereum foundation sells ETH and what
>Chainlink labs sells LINK and what

hypocrites and mind raped baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62143907
>>62143438
Someone post the alts chipmunk.

62144675
>>62143203
Seething linkbaggie
how is that flippening coming along zach?
https://link-flip.alwaysbeshipp.ing/

62144998
>>62143203
>chainlinkdog obsesses over biggest most obvious scam ever that does the same thing as the company he works for
interesting
would be a real shame if xrp went up in price especially compared to link
I wonder how they will cope then

62145068
>>62143560
so ripple buying prime brokerage and countless other institutional leaning companies to create their ecosystem is just pure dumping on the community? crypto tribal fags are so pathetic

62146230
>>62143438
>>62144998
>>62145068
Ripple dumps on TOKEN HOLDERS to fund things that DO NOT USE THE TOKEN, and explicitly only benefit SHAREHOLDERS.

Nobody else in crypto does this.
How are you people so insanely retarded.

62146371
>>62146230
Chainlink does the exact same thing you dense paid mouthbreather shill
None of the shit that CLL promotes or builds or advertizes has anything to do whit the token itself

62146402
>>62146371
If it uses CL nodes, it uses the token.
So to prove your position, all you have to do is name one CLL product that does not use the nodes.
Go

62146438
>>62146402
name one instance when you had to purchase the token
go

62146458
>>62146371
>it has nothing to do with the token

>>62146438
>ok it does need the token, but you didn't purchase it directly

woosh go the goalposts

62146481
>>62146458
>muh subsidies mean token needed despite the fact that absolutely no one buys it or uses it for anything
you're unironically a moron if you truly believe semantics and nuances are doing anything for you
you've been trying this angle for 6 years to no avail, read the room you spastic

62146485
>>62146371
>it has nothing to do with the token

>>62146438
>ok it does need the token, but you didn't purchase it directly

>>62146481
>ok purchasing directly or indirectly is irrelevant, but some of it is subsidized

Second goalpost move in a row.

62146502
>>62146485
not a single product requires the usage of LINK
no, subsidies do not matter at all, sorry
not a single trial or pilot requires the LINK token at any capacity, not a single institutional partner has ever touched the token
can't wait until you'll finally get doxed and raped in Minecraft

62146506
>>62146502
>not a single product requires the usage of LINK
All products use CL nodes, therefore requiring the usage of Link.

You're probably going to loop right back to "but you didn't purchase the Link directly".

>no, subsidies do not matter at all, sorry
But you're the one who brought up the subsidies.

What a shitty chatbot.

62146532
>>62146506
you do realize that once Chainlink implodes, you along with all the other paid marketeers will be in grave danger? you do realize that attacking your entire online persona around a crypto scamcoin will inevitably end bad for you?
tick, tock...

62146544
>>62146532
>paid marketeer
I'm not the one lying, moving goalposts, forgetting my own talking points, and changing IDs.

Go ahead, name one CL product that does not use CL nodes (and therefore the Link token).

62146565
>>62146506
kill yourself before I find you, Rajesh

62146578
>says some bullshit
>gets debunked
>changes IDs and gets extremely emotional

many such cases, sad!

62147333
>>62145068
>to create their ecosystem is just pure dumping on the community?
What ecosystem? The one they claim is coming? None of the companies they bought have integrated XRPL.

62147341
>>62143203
>dont come to /biz/ for a year
>its still the same fucking threads by cultist linkies and xrp schizos
We’ll see you next year

62147491
>>62147341
it's literally the same 4 retards in every fucking thread

62147758
>>62146458
literally the same can be said of xrp kek

62147964
Chainlink is such a pathetic little project...

62148311
>>62147341
next year chainlink will be dead and buried though, and the catalogue will be flooded with frogposters crying about "why didn't you tell me????"

62151224
>>62147758
Fucking how?

62154431
>>62146230
Ripple don't sell to retail and their products are building around actual use. Chainlink sell every green candle directly to retail and built zero utility for the token that is itself completely disconnected from the actual product.

62155143
>>62154431
>Ripple don't sell to retail
After the lawsuit and the SEC waiver, if Ripple wanted to sell to institutions they'd have to file an application with the SEC saying "we want to sell securities".

These applications are public, so anyone can verify that Ripple have never filed such an application to sell XRP to institutions: https://www.sec.gov/edgar/browse/?CIK=1685012
Thus they've been selling to retail exclusively.

>built zero utility for the token
See >>62146402
Link token utility is absolute; every single CL feature and product requires it.

Meanwhile Ripple pretty much only builds products and buys companies that have nothing inherently to do with XRP.

62155954
i made this thread
you are all spiritually indian
when indians get criticized they say “well pakistan is shittier!”. i dont care about chainlink, i dont invest in link, its probably a paypig investor scam too, i made this thread about xrp and you xrp schizos all had to create this charicture in ur mind about how i must be a link shill

no
of ur first thought after reading a criticism of xrp is “well at least we arent chainlink investors” then,
you are spiritually indian

62156263
>>62155143
None of you have ever been able to explain how that would cause the price of the fundraising token to increase. If node operators are paid in lank, then they immediately turn around and sell those tokens for actual money, doesn’t that just increase sell pressure? I’m also slightly retarded, so feel free to explain it to me

62156366
>>62156263
>If node operators are paid in lank, then they immediately turn around and sell those tokens for actual money, doesn’t that just increase sell pressure?
That's how blockchain miners have always operated, you dip.

62156399
>>62156366
oh so it's not the future

item.title: >nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi… — 54 replies — 66.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi…

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-04-25 19:17 UTC

OP

>nofap
>religion
>stock tips
>meditation
>therapy
>healing crystals
>self-help books
>pomodoro
>PDFs
>affirmations
>positive thinking
>DCA
>crypto
>"self improvement"

Early replies (first 3)

62143060
>/biz/: Business & Finance

62143073
Different ways of coping. No boomber ever had to read the meditations or go to the gym

62143145
>>62143073
the boomers who did DCA have generational wealth now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145001
>>62144995
>>62144996
Boom right on cue
They are generally pretty quick to reply as well because that kind of derangement generally stems from being terminally online

62145005
I've read
>how to win friends and influence people
>art of war
>12 rules by peterson
>48 laws of power
>atomic habits
>the art of not giving a fuck
>the art of the deal
>meditations of auruelius(stoic)
>4 hour work week
>the secret
>rich dad poor dad and the sequel

And yet my life still sucks. And before you say "but anon your'e supposed to do the things the books say to do not just read them!" well I have. For example richdad poordad says to borrow money from your friends and buy a house, flip it and then give them back their money-the exact amount they let you borrow. Well I asked all my friends for money and NONE of them gave me any. Only one even asked what it was for and when I told him he laughed in my face and said "You have to know how to flip a house to flip a house."

What the fuck

62145007
>>62145001
>>62144995
>generally pretty quick to reply
The irony. Also you have to be 18+ to post here

62145009
>>62144501
>the meaning to this life is uhhhh if you're a good boy and follow this book, you'll have an infinitely long life after this one, 100% guaranteed, and in that life everything will be great, okay?

62145415
>>62143054
>>nofap
Difficult to do, confers immediate health and psychological benefits, and every normie has a bizarre level hatred for it.

That should tell you something.

62145428
>>62144238
Of course stoicism is a cuck mentality. It's a philosophy of decadence. No Greek during the peak of their civilization would have created something like stoicism the same way no boomber would have been interested in the meditations by marcus aurelius.

When the real world becomes too painful, many people retire and create ways of coping.

62145464
>>62145428
Real stoicism is a an "emptying"/devotion of yourself to higher Being. This is a living, communicative path that taps you into energetic circuits and correspondence. Stoicism as most people know it isn't transformative in any higher sense.

62145495
>>62145464
Well, I meant decadence in the societal sense. Individuals live in a society. Stoicism was born exactly when the greek dream ended and that's why it became so popular. Same way as Epicureism.

And that's why it is so goddamn popular now and you have depressed 21 yo kids talking about stoicism. It has become a meme. You're depressed? Read The Meditations and go to the gym, bro. The real world has become painful and people have to find ways to deal with the real world.

ps. By the way, Seneca moral writings are way more interesting than Marcus Aurelius' Meditations

62145579
>>62145009
Correct
>t. full trust that I'm where I need to be when I need to be there

62145590
>>62145579
retardmaxxing with extra steps

62145626
>>62145590
I wish I could impart to you even 1% of the things I've seen, alas just know its sort of like a Chinese finger trap you know what I mean?
>retardmaxxing
Okay, okay! Based!

62145653
>>62143054
i went all in on long distance ejaculation training, sucks to suck, peace i gotta catch a flight, there are some ceilings that need coating

62145752
>>62145005
rich dad poor dad taught me to put all my money in pokemon cards

62147432
Izzat farming thread
Abandon ship

62147439
It costs zero dollars to not be so darned rude

62147451
>>62143054

fuck i forgot about pomodoro. imagine having a faggy little tomato timer on your desk as an adult

62148909
>>62144289
this

62151619
>>62144395
... if you want to end up in hell

62151984
>>62144435
Marcus Aurelius didn't trust the slut, he knew she was bad when he married her. Thing was, she was the daughter of the previous emperor who was a cool guy and Aurelius married her out of loyalty to him (plus he got to be emperor himself out of it).

62152046
>>62143054
for me its several different stimulants and an ssri
amazingly im still a husk of a human, its honestly impressive financially speaking

62152662
Scam

62152741
>>62143054
Many of those things have improved my life a lot. Of course, I am not a billionaire but I like to enjoy the little things in life

62152905
>>62145005
You're supposed to read good fiction and let your mind wander, not that (((self help))) slop.

62155066
>>62143054
tbqh deepest post on this board

62155320
>>62147439
opportunity cost of missing a zinger tho

62156116
stupid demoralizing thread

62156188
>>62152741
>Many of those things have improved my life a lot
like what

62156224
>>62144272
>>62144395
>t.

62156315
>>62143054
My coming has gotten out of control that I don't even try to do nofap anymore. From 16-21 I was extremely passionate about but now at 27 I unironically have multiple screens of degeneracy on play and I even plan out my sessions.

62156324
>>62143054
Ask yourself this. Do any richfag elitists like Bezos do any of this?

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 63 replies — 22.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-04-25 17:20 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62148933
>>62146529
Yeah it’s been brutal, but not all protocols are like that. Bitcoin itself is secured by a strong base layer design. It’s not about chasing yield, it’s about using BTC without breaking its core security or custody model.

62148936
Never tell anyone how much money you have. Poor, rich, anything.

62148947
>>62141874
What makes you think that?

62149370
>>62146661
Like >>62147680 it's was around $1000

62149399
>>62142226
Did you enjoy losing 3 mil, fucker?

62149941
>>62138453
Only my brother knows I bought BTC, we both stake together now as well, with native staking gaining traction. Imagine if I told my parents to buy at 120K Kek.

62150719
>>62142197
You never know

62150903
>>62148933
Still it has risks

62151073
>>62146671
I get that, but it’s better to check first before saying that. So far, it fits my standards. I don’t think firms like Ledger or even A16z would back it otherwise.

62151201
>>62148936
Greedy bastard

62151260
>>62151073
it's the same thing. Everything will fail in the end. Nothing survive

62151310
NEVER

62151368
>>62149941
Heartaches

62151461
>>62138453
How do we confess them?

62151818
>>62149941
Nice are you also waiting for the TBV launch? More interesting for me since it could unlock more use cases.

62152719
>>62142226
That's the goal lol

62152732
never ever tell anyone you own any crypto holy shit that is like rule #1 in life don't tell me you idiots are trying to orange pill your fucking friends and family

62152763
>>62138453
Why would you ever brag about your money? Especially when every cryptobro is a scrawny geek. Do you want to get tortured by niggers for your private key?

Secret wealthy is better

62153574
>>62150903
Every system has risk. The difference is just what kind of risk you’re exposed to and how much control you still keep over your assets.

62153589
>>62152732
Maybe it depends on who you’re closest to in your family. There’s usually one person you really vibe with.

62153929
>>62152763
Do what you wanna do

62154078
>>62142464
This here is the right play. Don't tell anyone how much money you have or flaunt it. It should be used to give you freedom not ego validation. Also when you reveal wealth to people they will try to use you and get some for themselves. Donate to charity and volunteer also though of course.

62154092
If you talk about bitcoin, people either assume you’re a sucker, a drug dealer, evading taxation, or a pedophile. I think what really limits its reach is that it’s technology, economics and political philosophy wrapped into an asset. Never selling.

62155160
>>62142464
Well...she's still your gf so do that...

62155300
>>62154092
Think the opposite

62155673
>>62155300
What’s the opposite?

62155774
I haven't told anyone how much I have invested, I just tell them my various investment schemes so they hopefully do something.
It's especially important for the kids because their zog parents tell them not to invest and to get a car for MUH FREEDOMS.

62155914
>>62151310
Ok, that's not a problem

62156005
Guys check out my crypto currency called hsgold I’m going to add a fair bit of liquidity at the end of the month when I get paid check it out highseasgold currently swapable for sol

62156014
>>62138453
That's RULE NUMBER ONE of the Ten Crack Commandments anon.

item.title: DO YOU KNOW ANYONE WHO LOST MONEY ON MEMECOINS — 21 replies — 17.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
DO YOU KNOW ANYONE WHO LOST MONEY ON MEMECOINS

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-04-25 18:55 UTC

OP

DO YOU KNOW ANYONE WHO LOST MONEY ON MEMECOINS

Early replies (first 3)

62154328
Yeah

62154329
>>62154323
Almost everyone lost money on memecoins just like how she lost all her gains

62154342
>>62154323
I want her to squeeze my head like a pumpkin in between her thighs, if you catch my meaning.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62154347
>>62154323
I know a guy. I also knew a guy who lost his money on futures. Same story for both. Decided they'd rather day trade from a bar than get a real job. Wounds up broke, alone, and alcoholics.

62154361
>>62154323
memecoins are made for losing money. you have to balance out your capital gains somehow

62154373
>>62154323
Me. Is this a trick question? This is /biz/, most people here lost money on memecoins

62154377
>>62154361
This. You need to get some losses out of your system. Need to get a cheap thrill once in a while with some retarded small bets so that you stay rational with your core folio

62154462
>>62154347
>>62154329
>>62154328
>>62154373
are you guys being serious or are you trolling?

Almost everyone on x that I see is still hyped on memecoins
Can you share more stories? I'm not part of gen z and don't really know what it's like on the ground for you guys

62154473
>>62154323
>yeah lost money last few days
>still losing
>she's cute ngl
>arms are a bit much not my thing

62154481
I know him, and he is me.

62154731
>>62154323
My fren boat trump coin at quite high 2 digit price, i told him to sell the first dead cat but he didnt....

62154753
>>62154462
Yeah

62154757
>>62154323
No I don't ever lose money

62154833
>>62154323
if i encounter anyone who speaks of memecoins i un know them

62154933
>>62154323

62155966
>>62154323
NW needed to date her?

62156010
>>62154323
>>62154342
>>62154473
least attractive sex wrestler. here is a real woman worth rasslin with.

62156018
>>62154323
No, but everyone I know lost money on coins with utility and fundamentals.

62156163
>>62154323
Yes. I have a friend on Facebook who ate shit when bitcoin crashed.

62156176
>>62156010

62156269
>>62156163
Remember that one dude who bet like 6 BTC on Trump winning the election? I thought he was a genius because it seemed like a sure bet, free money! He never did post a follow up...

item.title: Remote Work Routes for Electrical Engineers — 9 replies — 9.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Remote Work Routes for Electrical Engineers

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-25 19:11 UTC

OP

People who work from home with an engineering degree, what do you do?

I'm graduating in a year and can't bring myself to deal with corporate BS after doing 2 internships, which both felt like hell specifically because of the corporate environment. I'd finish my projects weeks before they needed to be done and could feel my precious time being sapped away. Needing to "find more work" to do with no additional pay incentive is the most cucked thing ever and needs to be avoided at all costs. I'm sure most of you can relate to that.

Anyone with an engineering degree here that does freelance/remote work where they can spend a day max grinding out projects and get the rest of the week to themselves? Job stacking freelance type roles seems like the play for someone who gets shit done and doesn't suck off upper management from their wage cells all day.

Early replies (first 3)

62155809
>>62155791
Im a ECE with 3 years in industry. Been applying to remote stuff ~50 applications for the past year and havent gotten an interview. Market is a lot tougher for remote work then normal jobs and most of the remote positions are for more people with 8+ years of experiance. Its rough in our field since otherwise you would need to move far for work.

good luck

62155813
I know of an electrical engineer that offers consultations.

62155855
>>62155809
might I ask what you're currently doing? Im looking for a role where my time is valued. Getting paid for being another body in the office is my nightmare.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62155953
>>62155855
I do "Design for Test" work. Anytime you get a salaried position like i got your time isn't going to be valued by the employer. You need to value it yourself by not working overtime. If you're not willing to work overtime you'll need to be good at your job and even then you may need to every now and then. Depending on the company you can leave early some days when you get your stuff done.

100% aim to get a remote job, thats my goal but just because i dont want to live in a city.

62155999
An European EE here. Wfh most of the time. Pick an industry and grind in it until you become efficient to a point where they dont need to keep an eye on you. It took me something like 8-9 years. Maybe you can do it faster. I've started in design, moved on to compliance and technical assurance, then moved on to another sector. Now comfy. Good luck. You only get what you build yourself, noone will give you anything for free...

62156030
You can make.a vaporware company and get people to invest fully remote. Just make it about space or quantum so musktards will buy it with zero proof of concept.

62156216
>>62155791
kek thanks for reminding me about how fucking stupid zoomshits are. You are not getting a remote job with no real experience. You will learn some basic social skills and give up the zoomer stare

62156306
>>62156216
You're clearly a retard because you didn't even read my post. I have experience. I know enough about the industry to realize I can contribute more when I have control of my time and resources. You zogslaves think it's dignifying to sit in a wage cell for weeks on end acting busy when they could do their job in a few hours and live life.

Social skills are for cucked losers that think people care about them. kek kek kek ngmi

You will feed zog. You will like it. You will talk about the weather.

62156310
>>62156216
>You will learn some basic social skills and give up the zoomer stare

picrel
